authors_affiliation: Ao Ran, Dongsheng Ruan, Yuan Zheng, Huafeng Liu; Zhejiang University, China

result.evaluation_split: Unable to score/rank on the official hidden validation and test sets; results reported via 5-fold cross-validation on the training set

self_reported_metrics.other: 5-fold CV challenge score of 0.7030 ± 0.0090 for the proposed EASTNet model (best among compared models: SVM 0.5611 ± 0.0156, LSTM 0.5937 ± 0.0119, 1D-ResNet34 0.6760 ± 0.0044, 1D-ResNet34+SEblock 0.6856 ± 0.0132, 1D-ResNet34+MLP 0.6875 ± 0.0121)

model.architecture_details: EASTNet: a 34-layer 1D ResNet (modified from 2D image ResNet) with 1D Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) blocks embedded in each residual basic block, followed by a multi-layer perceptron (MLP) classification head (512 -> 256 -> 24 nodes). First convolution kernel enlarged to size 15 to increase receptive field, followed by a 7x7 convolution; max pooling replaced with a strided convolutional layer; dropout layers added for robustness.
model.key_novelty: Combines a heartbeat-level random sampling data augmentation strategy (concatenating several randomly selected single-heartbeat segments as one input) with a 1D SE-ResNet feature encoder whose SE blocks let the network learn inter-lead channel importance.

input.signal_length_window: Input formed from multiple intercepted single-heartbeat segments (four randomly sampled beats used as the final input after comparing 1-6 beats)
input.sampling_rate: not reported (each intercepted heartbeat resampled to 300 sample points)
input.preprocessing: Baseline drift removal, 3-45 Hz FIR band-pass filtering, R-peak detection, heartbeat interception around the R-peak, resampling of each beat segment to 300 points, then random sampling/combination of heartbeats as input

data.training_datasets: Official challenge training set only, filtered/cleaned to 6720 valid records (201600 extracted heartbeats); no external data reported
data.class_imbalance_handling: Statistics collected per abnormality class, then selective discarding and duplication of records to balance the class distribution as much as possible
data.augmentation_techniques: Random combination of intercepted single-heartbeat segments (compared 1-6 beats, both continuous and random sampling; 4 random beats chosen as final input) used explicitly as a data augmentation method that reduces overfitting

training.cross_validation_strategy: 5-fold cross-validation on the training set (also compared against 3-fold and 4-fold CV for robustness)

contribution.summary: Proposes EASTNet, a 1D SE-ResNet+MLP model trained on randomly sampled/combined single-heartbeat segments rather than whole ECG records, and shows via 5-fold CV that this beat-sampling augmentation reduces overfitting and outperforms SVM, LSTM, and plain 1D-ResNet34 baselines, though the team could not obtain an official leaderboard score/rank.
